methodsAttempted.query: site:pte.hu (ChatGPT OR "generative AI" OR "artificial intelligence" OR "mesterséges intelligencia" OR "generatív mesterséges intelligencia") (policy OR guideline OR szabályzat OR oktatás OR hallgató)
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official University of Pecs/PTE AI policy and guidance PDFs on pte.hu subdomains, plus the central PTE AI landing page.
Robots.txt for kpvk.pte.hu did not disallow the official PDF path and requested a crawl delay; one policy-specific PDF was fetched.
Robots.txt for pte.hu allowed the public AI landing page; the page described Smart University AI work but did not itself state AI-use requirements for students or assessment.

artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery located the University of Pecs central Hungarian AI guideline PDF for educational and research activities connected to study requirements. The general AI landing page was recorded as an official but low-specificity discovery result and rejected as claim evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official PTE-subdomain PDF titled as the University of Pecs Artificial Intelligence Guideline, signed by the rector and scoped to AI use connected with educational and research activities needed for study requirements.

artifacts.sourceTitle: A Pécsi Tudományegyetem Mesterséges Intelligencia Irányelve

artifacts.claimText: The University of Pecs AI guideline is scoped to educational and research activities needed to meet study requirements and applies to university citizens when they use AI systems in connection with setting, meeting, or evaluating course and training requirements.

artifacts.claimText: For student work, the University of Pecs guideline advises students who use AI-generated text to check its content and cite it as AI-produced text according to TVSZ Annex 14 and the citation form recommended in the guideline.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow scope claim; the guideline text is advisory and should not be framed as a binding prohibition.
Approved because the snippet directly states checking and citation guidance for AI-generated text; wording uses advises rather than requires.
Approved as privacy and security guidance for students entering data into AI systems.
Approved because the snippet directly states the university does not recommend automated decision systems or large language models for substantive qualifying evaluation.

artifacts.title: University of Pecs AI Guideline Candidate Claims
artifacts.summary: Staged candidate claims from the University of Pecs central Hungarian AI guideline. The source frames the document as guidance and recommendations, not a prescriptive regulation, so claim wording should remain advisory unless later human review identifies stronger binding language.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The guideline is in Hungarian and explicitly describes itself as recommendations rather than a regulation containing prescriptive requirements.
Faculty-specific PDF policies surfaced during discovery were not used for university-wide claims in this bundle.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
